description_long: This is a highly ornate stone obelisk integrated into a brick wall, featuring elaborate baroque-style carvings and reliefs on its front face. The central section is adorned with a large, shield-shaped plaque surrounded by intricate floral and scrollwork motifs, flanked by cherubic figures and decorative garlands. The upper portion is crowned with a broken pediment and additional sculptural elements, giving it a grand and historical appearance. The base is robust and rectangular, with recessed panels and a projecting plinth, providing a solid foundation. The sides and rear are less decorated, with exposed brickwork visible, indicating the obelisk is partially embedded in a wall. The overall color palette is dominated by pale gray stone and reddish-brown brick, with weathering and patina adding to its aged character.
description: An ornate stone obelisk with baroque carvings and a shield-shaped plaque, partially embedded in a brick wall. The base is solid and rectangular, with elaborate reliefs and decorative motifs on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed baroque carvings, central plaque, cherubs, and ornate upper pediment.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the relief work and the exposed reddish-brown brick wall.
description_view_2: Rear-angled view highlighting the back of the obelisk, mostly plain with visible brickwork and minimal decoration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the brick wall integration and the profile of the sculpted front.
attribute_inscription: There appears to be a shield-shaped plaque on the front, possibly intended for an inscription, but the text is not legible in the images.
attribute_ornamentation: Extensive baroque-style ornamentation on the front, including floral and scrollwork reliefs, cherubic figures, garlands, and a broken pediment at the top.
attribute_base_design: The base is rectangular and robust, featuring recessed panels and a projecting plinth, all crafted from stone with some decorative molding.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is not a traditional pointed obelisk but is topped with a broken pediment and sculptural elements, adding to the ornate baroque style.
attribute_surrounding_features: The obelisk is partially embedded in a brick wall, with no other notable surrounding features visible.
